attack_chain.description: Threat actor used previously compromised credentials to authenticate to the customer's VPN environment, gaining access to the AWS Private Cloud.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: use of two likely compromised credentials to connect to the customer's Virtual Private Network (VPN) environment

attack_chain.description: Performed internal reconnaissance of AWS environment, later using Nmap from a domain controller instance and a newly joined device.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: threat actor performed internal reconnaissance activities

evidence_chunks.quote: threat actor performed internal reconnaissance activities
threat actor performed internal reconnaissance activities

attack_chain.description: Staged the Rclone tool (rclone-v1.69.0-windows-amd64.zip) on an AWS EC2 instance associated with a PKI service.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: staged the Rclone tool "ProgramData\rclone-v1.69.0-windows-amd64.zip" ... to an AWS instance whose hostname is associated with a public key infrastructure (PKI) service

attack_chain.description: Accessed and downloaded multiple finance and investment-related files from an AWS file server instance.

attack_chain.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Asymmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
attack_chain.description: PKI EC2 instance initiated SSH connections using Rclone SSH client to RockHoster VPS endpoint 193.242.184[.]178 to exfiltrate collected data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: PKI-related EC2 instance started making SSH connections with the Rclone SSH client "SSH-2.0-rclone/v1.69.0" to a RockHoster Virtual Private Server (VPS) endpoint (193.242.184[.]178)

attack_chain.description: After initial exfil blocked, continued scanning using Nmap from a separate AWS domain controller instance and newly joined device; downloaded ~5GB more from file server.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: threat actor continued to conduct network scans using the Nmap tool, operating from both a separate AWS domain controller instance and a newly joined device

evidence_chunks.quote: threat actor continued to conduct network scans using the Nmap tool, operating from both a separate AWS domain controller instance and a newly joined device
threat actor continued to conduct network scans using the Nmap tool, operating from both a separate AWS domain controller instance and a newly joined device

attack_chain.description: Lateral movement via unusual internal RDP connections to an AWS printer EC2 instance to pivot and strengthen foothold.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Unusual internal RDP connections to a likely AWS printer instance indicated that the threat actor was looking to ... pivot to other devices

attack_chain.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Asymmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
attack_chain.description: AWS printer instance initiated SSH to alternative endpoint 67.217.57[.]252 (Host Department VPS, previously linked to Akira ransomware) after original destination blocked.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: AWS printer instance initiating SSH communication with a rare external endpoint ... (67.217.57[.]252)

initial_access_vector: Previously compromised credentials used to connect to the customer's VPN, gaining access to AWS Private Cloud environment.
persistence_mechanism: Continued use of valid VPN credentials; lateral RDP pivoting to alternative hosts after containment.
